title: Cyanea rollandia
intro: Cyanea rollandia, also known as the Rolland's halapeha, is a rare and endangered flowering plant species endemic to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cyanea rollandia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues.
